# Interventional cardiology articles from across Nature Portfolio

Interventional cardiology is the use of intravascular, catheter-based techniques in the treatment of patients with cardiovascular disease.

## Latest Research and Reviews

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs41598-025-15213-3/MediaObjects/41598_2025_15213_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [Retrospective evaluation of the predictive value of acute pacing capture threshold for long-term outcomes in Chinese patients with leadless pacemakers](/articles/s41598-025-15213-3)

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs41598-025-13765-y/MediaObjects/41598_2025_13765_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [Crossover balloon occlusion technique for access closure during transcatheter aortic valve implantation: a multicenter observational study](/articles/s41598-025-13765-y)

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs41598-025-10932-z/MediaObjects/41598_2025_10932_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [Impact of sex on the outcome of troponin-positive patients with non-obstructive coronary arteries](/articles/s41598-025-10932-z)

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs41598-025-08038-7/MediaObjects/41598_2025_8038_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [Cardiorespiratory fitness after correction of pectus excavatum: a systematic review with meta-analysis](/articles/s41598-025-08038-7)

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs41598-025-11968-x/MediaObjects/41598_2025_11968_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [Long-term prognosis after acute coronary syndrome due to *de novo* coronary artery lesions and stent thrombosis in patients on hemodialysis](/articles/s41598-025-11968-x)

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs41467-025-61916-6/MediaObjects/41467_2025_61916_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [Colchicine in patients with aortic stenosis undergoing transcatheter aortic valve replacement: a double-blind randomized trial](/articles/s41467-025-61916-6)

An inflammatory process may increase the risk of arrhythmias after transcatheter aortic valve replacement. Here, the authors show that periprocedural treatment with colchicine may reduce the incidence of new-onset arrhythmias and subclinical leaflet thrombosis after transcatheter aortic valve replacement.

![Advertisement](//pubads.g.doubleclick.net/gampad/ad?iu=/285/subjects/interventional-cardiology&sz=300x250&c=-1753805816&t=pos%3Darticle-list%26artid%3D)

## News and Comment

### [Pulsed field ablation non-inferior to cryoablation for paroxysmal AF](/articles/s41569-025-01159-6)

In patients with symptomatic paroxysmal atrial fibrillation, pulsed field ablation is non-inferior to cryoballoon ablation for preventing the recurrence of atrial tachyarrhythmia, according to findings from the SINGLE SHOT CHAMPION trial.

### [No effect of cerebral embolic protection devices on stroke risk after TAVI](/articles/s41569-025-01156-9)

Findings from the BHF PROTECT-TAVI trial indicate that the routine use of cerebral embolic protection devices during transcatheter aortic valve implantation does not decrease the incidence of stroke within 72 h after the intervention.

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs41569-025-01137-y/MediaObjects/41569_2025_1137_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [Clinical implications of perioperative and periprocedural myocardial infarction](/articles/s41569-025-01137-y)

Differing definitions and questionable prognostic significance have caused confusion and controversy around the applicability of the current definitions of periprocedural and perioperative myocardial infarction in clinical practice. In this Clinical Outlook, we review the definitions and the clinical and prognostic relevance of the various entities and provide implications for clinical practice.

### [Combining TAVI with PCI in patients with aortic stenosis and CAD](/articles/s41569-024-01080-4)

In the NOTION-3 trial, percutaneous coronary intervention reduced the occurrence of major adverse cardiac events compared with conservative treatment in patients who were undergoing transcatheter aortic valve implantation for severe aortic stenosis and who had stable coronary artery disease.

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs41569-024-01089-9/MediaObjects/41569_2024_1089_Figa_HTML.png)

### [TEER is non-inferior to surgery in patients with secondary mitral regurgitation](/articles/s41569-024-01089-9)

In patients with heart failure and secondary mitral valve regurgitation, transcatheter edge-to-edge repair is non-inferior to mitral valve surgery with respect to a composite of death, hospitalization for heart failure, reintervention, implantation of an assist device or stroke at 1 year after the procedure, according to findings from the MATTERHORN trial.

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs41569-024-01059-1/MediaObjects/41569_2024_1059_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [Transcatheter treatment of pure aortic regurgitation](/articles/s41569-024-01059-1)

Preliminary experience with the use of transcatheter aortic valve implantation (TAVI) to treat non-calcific aortic regurgitation has raised concerns about the short-term effectiveness of TAVI in this setting. A deeper understanding of the interaction between transcatheter heart valves and anatomy in patients with non-calcific aortic valve disease, coupled with the introduction of dedicated TAVI devices, is providing new opportunities in the management of this condition.

enriched_points.expanded_content: A comprehensive review of current literature, including articles from JACC and Nature, highlights new techniques and outcomes in interventional cardiology, influencing practice and guidelines.
Evaluating data from key clinical trials, such as the NOTION-3 and BHF PROTECT-TAVI, reveals insights into procedural efficacy and safety for diverse patient populations.
Conducting interviews with leading cardiologists provides firsthand perspectives on emerging interventions, challenges faced in practice, and the future direction of cardiovascular care.
